title: rhododendron subflumineum
intro: Rhododendron subflumineum, also known as the subalpine rhododendron, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the rhododendron genus and is native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest, USA. The plant's flowers and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Rhododendron subflumineum include its ability to treat fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Rhododendron subflumineum is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and may be used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
